what_it_does: This file is an IIFE that exposes window.forkBrainCore, a single reusable client-side brain-turn engine. It POSTs to /api/claude/turn and parses an NDJSON stream, maintains per-address session_id continuity, strips markdown before display, maps opaque server-side pointable tokens back to local UI addresses, and provides a batched, fail-loud, verification-aware writer that POSTs to /api/territory/write and emits gallery:rerender or gallery:write-error events.
observations: Uses an IIFE and attaches a single global object window.forkBrainCore.
Streams NDJSON from /api/claude/turn using fetch + ReadableStream + TextDecoder.
Keeps a private _sessions map keyed by address to continue conversations.
Strips markdown syntax from every emitted text block as a display-layer safety net.
Reads a live pointables catalog from window.surfacePointables and sends only {token,label} to the server.
Maps returned point tokens back to local addresses and dispatches ui:point CustomEvents.
Provides writeDirections that batches items by element_id and POSTs each batch to /api/territory/write.
Suppresses all writes when the URL contains ?verify, emitting gallery:write-suppressed events.
Fail-loud: emits gallery:write-error on non-2xx or network failures and surfaces brain errors in the reply element.
standouts: The verification-mode chokepoint on the route-back write path.
Markdown stripping applied uniformly to both default and custom onText rendering.
Opaque pointable tokens: server sees token/label only; address resolution stays client-side.
Single-source brain engine intended to be mounted by both gallery-unit and V/RHM consumers.
conventions: IIFE with a window global export.
Async/await with fetch and streaming response body.
CustomEvent-based surface communication (ui:point, gallery:rerender, gallery:write-error, gallery:write-suppressed).
Defensive try/catch wrappers around DOM and URL interactions.
Operator-law comments that keep addresses client-side.
concerns: _sessions is an unbounded plain object with no eviction or persistence across reloads.
Markdown stripping regexes may over-process or accidentally alter legitimate text.
talk() does not check resp.ok before reading the stream.
No AbortController, so in-flight turns cannot be cancelled.
Relies on window.surfacePointables and a ui:point consumer existing elsewhere; not self-contained.
Verification detection is only URL-parameter based and wrapped in a broad try/catch that silently falls back to real writes on error.
notes: Central surface brain-turn and route-back core that enforces plain-prose display, opaque pointables, and verification-guarded persistence.
questions: What exact NDJSON event types and fields does /api/claude/turn emit beyond init/text/tool/point/done/error?
How and where is window.surfacePointables registered, and what is its catalog shape?
What components consume gallery:rerender, gallery:write-error, and gallery:write-suppressed events?
What is the expected schema for items passed to writeDirections?
How is the V/RHM mount supposed to supply the current aim address to talk()?
Is there a server-side or client-side limit on pointables count or prompt length?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: a single reusable brain-turn engine with per-address session continuity, NDJSON streaming, pointable handling, and a verification-aware route-back writer.
apparent_themes: single source of truth for brain turns
intention_signals: The file is trying to consolidate two surface brain mounts into one shared engine while preventing verification automation from persisting data and ensuring the model's rendered voice stays plain prose.
novelty: Combines NDJSON streaming, markdown sanitization, opaque local pointable token mapping, and URL-guarded write suppression in one client-side core module.
contribution: A reusable client-side brain-turn orchestrator that streams responses, manages per-address sessions, sanitizes displayed text, maps server-opaque pointable tokens to local UI addresses, and provides a batched, fail-loud, verification-aware persistence writer.
summary_for_embedding: fork-brain-core.js is an IIFE exposing window.forkBrainCore, a reusable client-side brain-turn engine that streams NDJSON from /api/claude/turn, maintains per-address session continuity, strips markdown for plain-prose display, dispatches local pointable tokens via ui:point, and batches fail-loud verification-guarded writes through /api/territory/write.
intention_for_embedding: Provide one shared, surface-local brain engine for both gallery-unit and V/RHM mounts, enforcing operator-law address opacity, verification-safe persistence, and clean spoken-voice rendering.

symbols.does: Strips common markdown syntax from a string so the rendered voice stays plain prose.
symbols.description: A pure helper that removes code fences, inline code, bold, italic, headers, and bullet markers from text while preserving the underlying prose and newlines. It is used as a display-layer safety net before every text emit.

symbols.does: Sends a prompt to /api/claude/turn and streams the NDJSON reply into a reply element or custom handler.
symbols.description: An async function that builds a request with per-address session_id and optional pointables, reads the NDJSON stream, accumulates text with soft breaks between distinct runs, handles init/point/done/error events, dispatches ui:point for pointable tokens, and surfaces errors fail-loud.

symbols.does: Wraps the configured text emitter with markdown stripping before output.
symbols.description: A local closure that applies _stripMd to every text value and then calls either the custom opts.onText or the default replyEl.textContent assignment, ensuring no markdown leaks to the surface.

symbols.does: Toggles a brain-loading CSS class on the reply element during the wait state.
symbols.description: A small helper that adds or removes the 'brain-loading' class on replyEl while the turn is waiting for the first token, used for styling a subtle animated ellipsis.

symbols.does: Detects whether the page URL contains a ?verify query parameter.
symbols.description: A defensive helper that returns true when window.location.search includes the 'verify' parameter; on any error it falls back to false so real writes are never accidentally blocked.

symbols.does: Batches direction items by element_id and POSTs them to /api/territory/write, guarded by verification mode.
symbols.description: A function that groups items per element, suppresses all writes in ?verify mode with gallery:write-suppressed events, otherwise posts each batch, emits gallery:rerender on success, and emits gallery:write-error with the server's reason on failure.
